messages.content: You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
How do I get started with Promptless?

To get started with Promptless:

1. **Create a free account** at accounts.gopromptless.ai
2. **Complete the six-step setup wizard**, which walks you through:
   - Connecting Slack (for notifications and @mention triggers)
   - Connecting your docs repository (GitHub or other Git providers)
   - Configuring triggers (which events kick off documentation suggestions)
   - Adding optional context sources (Linear, Jira, or Confluence)
   - Setting agent preferences (how Promptless behaves)
   - Starting your 30-day free trial

A few things worth knowing before you begin:
- Some steps require admin-level access to your tools (Slack, GitHub, Jira/Confluence, or Linear). If you don't have those permissions, you can invite a teammate with the right access to complete those steps.
- Your progress saves automatically, so you can stop and resume at any time.
- Setup doesn't have to be done by one person — different teammates can complete different steps based on their permissions.

After setup, Promptless indexes your docs (takes 5–30 minutes depending on repo size), then starts monitoring for trigger events and generating documentation suggestions for your review.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What exactly does Promptless do?

Promptless automatically generates and updates documentation based on your existing development and product workflows — keeping docs fresh, accurate, and in sync with your product.

It works through three core mechanisms:

- **Intelligent triggers**: Monitors your development ecosystem for events that signal a documentation need — things like pull requests, code changes, Slack messages, or support tickets.
- **Contextual understanding**: Integrates with your project management tools, code repos, and team communications to understand your organization's terminology and documentation style.
- **Multi-platform publishing**: Pushes updates across your documentation ecosystem, including GitHub-synced platforms and help centers.

In practice, this means when an engineer merges a PR, Promptless can detect the change, understand what it affects, and generate a documentation suggestion — without anyone having to remember to update the docs manually.

It's designed for both development teams (to eliminate documentation debt) and technical writers (to handle routine maintenance so they can focus on higher-level work).

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What permissions do I need to connect all the integrations?

Permission requirements vary by integration:

| Integration | Required Permission |
|---|---|
| **Slack** | Workspace admin (to install the Promptless app) |
| **GitHub** | Organization admin, or sufficient repository access to install the GitHub App |
| **Jira / Confluence** | Atlassian admin (to authorize the OAuth connection) |
| **Linear** | Workspace admin |

**Don't have the right access?** That's fine — you can invite a teammate who does during the onboarding process. They'll receive an email directing them to the same setup page and can complete that specific step on your behalf. Setup progress saves automatically, so you can pick up where they left off.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
I'm not a Slack admin. Does that mean I can't set up Promptless?

Not at all. During setup you can invite a teammate who has Slack workspace admin permissions to complete that step for you. They'll receive an email with a link to the same onboarding page and can pick up right where you left off.

Your setup progress is saved automatically, so you can complete all the steps you have access to, hand off the Slack connection to your Slack admin, and then continue from where they finish.

This same pattern applies to any other integration where you lack the required permissions — GitHub, Jira/Confluence, or Linear.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
Which documentation platforms does Promptless support?

Promptless works with GitHub repositories that use **docs-as-code** frameworks. Supported platforms include:

- ReadMe
- Mintlify
- Fern
- Docusaurus
- And more (additional platforms are added regularly)

The common thread is that your docs live in a Git repository — Promptless connects to that repo and pushes suggestions as pull requests or commits, fitting naturally into a code-review workflow.

If you're unsure whether your documentation setup is compatible, you can reach out to the team at help@gopromptless.ai.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What events can trigger Promptless to generate documentation suggestions?

Promptless supports two main trigger types:

**Automatic triggers (GitHub / Bitbucket)**
- Pull requests — when a PR is opened or merged, Promptless analyzes the changes and generates relevant documentation suggestions automatically.

**Manual triggers (Slack)**
- @mentions — you can mention Promptless in a Slack message to manually kick off a documentation suggestion.
- Message actions — trigger suggestions directly from Slack messages.

You configure which of these you want active during Step 3 of the setup wizard. You can enable one or both trigger types depending on your team's workflow.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
Can multiple people on my team work on the setup together?

Yes, Promptless is designed with collaborative setup in mind.

Setup progress saves automatically after every step, so the wizard doesn't need to be completed in one session or by one person. You can invite teammates during onboarding, and any invited teammate can continue from the current step.

A common pattern for teams:
- A developer or DevOps engineer connects GitHub and configures triggers.
- A Slack admin connects the Slack integration.
- A product manager or Atlassian admin connects Jira or Confluence.
- Anyone can finish the remaining steps and start the trial.

Invited teammates receive an email that takes them directly to the setup page.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
If I close the browser halfway through setup, will I lose my progress?

No — your progress saves automatically after each step. When you sign back in, you'll pick up exactly where you left off.

This also means you can start setup, pause to track down a colleague with the right admin permissions, and resume later without having to redo any steps.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What happens after I finish the setup wizard?

Once you complete the wizard and start your trial, Promptless begins indexing your documentation repository. This initial indexing takes **5 to 30 minutes**, depending on the size of your docs.

After indexing completes, Promptless will:
1. **Listen for trigger events** from the sources you configured (PRs, Slack @mentions, etc.)
2. **Analyze changes** and gather relevant context from your connected integrations
3. **Generate documentation suggestions** and surface them for your team to review

You'll receive a confirmation message — "You're all set! Promptless is now ready to help automate your documentation updates" — when everything is live.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
Is there a free trial? How long does it last?

Yes — Promptless offers a **30-day free trial** that you activate as the final step of the setup wizard. No credit card is mentioned as required to start.

For details on what's included in the trial or what happens when it ends, I'd recommend checking the pricing page or reaching out to the team at help@gopromptless.ai.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What's the point of the 'Additional Context' step in the setup wizard? Can I skip it?

The **Additional Context** step (Step 4) lets you connect optional integrations — Linear, Jira, Confluence, or Slack — so Promptless has richer context when generating documentation suggestions.

For example:
- Connecting **Jira or Linear** lets Promptless reference the ticket or issue associated with a code change, giving it background on *why* a feature was built.
- Connecting **Confluence** gives Promptless access to internal knowledge that may inform how something should be documented.

This step is entirely optional. You can skip it and still get functional documentation suggestions — the integrations just improve suggestion quality and relevance. You can also add them later if you want to start simple and expand over time.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
How long does the initial setup take from start to finish?

The six-step setup wizard itself is relatively quick — most teams complete it in under 30 minutes, though the actual time depends on how readily available the required admin credentials are.

The main source of delay is usually **permission gathering**: if different team members control Slack, GitHub, and Atlassian, you may need to coordinate across multiple people.

After the wizard is complete, Promptless indexes your documentation repository, which takes an additional **5 to 30 minutes** depending on the size of your docs. You don't need to do anything during this time — it runs in the background.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What integrations does Promptless support overall?

Promptless integrates with a wide range of tools:

**Source control / triggers**
- GitHub
- GitHub Enterprise
- Bitbucket

**Project management / context**
- Linear
- Atlassian (Jira + Confluence)

**Communication**
- Slack
- Microsoft Teams

**Support**
- Zendesk
- Intercom *(Beta)*

New integrations are added regularly — the team adds new ones every week. If you need something not on this list, reach out at help@gopromptless.ai.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
Is Promptless secure? Does it meet any compliance standards?

Yes — Promptless is **SOC 2 compliant**. It uses OAuth for authentication, role-based access controls, and encryption.

For detailed information about their security practices, privacy policy, or to discuss enterprise security requirements, I'd recommend reviewing the Security & Privacy section of the Promptless documentation or contacting the team directly at help@gopromptless.ai.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
Where do I create my Promptless account?
You can sign up for a free account at **accounts.gopromptless.ai**. Account creation is the first step before you begin the setup wizard.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
I finished setup 20 minutes ago but Promptless hasn't generated any suggestions yet. Is something wrong?

Probably not — this is expected. After setup, Promptless needs to **index your documentation repository** before it can start generating suggestions. That process takes **5 to 30 minutes** depending on how large your docs are.

If it's been less than 30 minutes, just give it a bit more time.

Once indexing is complete, Promptless will start listening for trigger events. Keep in mind that suggestions are generated in response to triggers — so if no PRs have been opened and no Slack @mentions have been made since setup, you won't see suggestions yet even after indexing finishes.

If it's been over 30 minutes and you've had trigger events fire with no suggestions appearing, reach out to help@gopromptless.ai.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
We use Bitbucket instead of GitHub. Does Promptless work with that?

Yes, Promptless supports Bitbucket as a trigger source. You can configure Bitbucket pull requests as automatic triggers during Step 3 of the setup wizard, the same way you would with GitHub.

For connecting your documentation repository (Step 2), if your docs also live in Bitbucket, I'd recommend confirming compatibility with the Promptless team at help@gopromptless.ai — the documentation specifically mentions GitHub and "other Git providers" for the docs connection.

You are a helpful support assistant for Promptless, an AI-powered documentation automation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely. When you don't know something, say so clearly rather than guessing.
What does the 'Agent preferences' step configure?

The **Agent preferences** step (Step 5) is where you configure how Promptless behaves when creating documentation suggestions — things like its style, tone, and approach.

For the specific options available in this step, I don't have the full details handy. The Promptless documentation's Configuration section should cover this in depth, or you can reach out to help@gopromptless.ai for guidance.
What's Promptless?

Promptless is an AI-powered documentation automation platform. It monitors your development workflows — pull requests, code changes, Slack messages, support tickets — and automatically generates documentation suggestions to keep your docs accurate and up to date.

Instead of engineers or technical writers having to remember to update docs after every change, Promptless detects when documentation likely needs updating and surfaces a suggestion for the team to review.
How do I trigger Promptless manually from Slack?

You can trigger Promptless from Slack in two ways:

1. **@mention Promptless** in a Slack message — this manually kicks off a documentation suggestion based on the context of that message.
2. **Slack message actions** — trigger a suggestion directly from an existing Slack message using Slack's message action menu.

For either of these to work, the Slack integration needs to be connected during setup (Step 1 of the setup wizard), and Slack triggers need to be enabled in your trigger configuration (Step 3).
walk me through the setup wizard step by step

Here's what each step of the Promptless setup wizard covers:

**Step 1 — Connect Slack**
Install the Promptless app in your Slack workspace. This enables Slack notifications and lets your team trigger documentation suggestions via @mentions. Requires Slack workspace admin permissions.

**Step 2 — Connect docs**
Link the Git repository where your documentation lives (GitHub or other Git providers). Promptless supports docs-as-code platforms like ReadMe, Mintlify, Fern, and Docusaurus. Requires GitHub organization admin access or sufficient repository permissions.

**Step 3 — Configure triggers**
Choose which events will automatically kick off documentation suggestions. Options include GitHub/Bitbucket pull requests (automatic) and Slack @mentions (manual).

**Step 4 — Additional context** *(optional)*
Connect Linear, Jira, Confluence, or Slack so Promptless has richer context — ticket descriptions, internal wikis, team discussions — when generating suggestions. Skippable; you can add these later.

**Step 5 — Agent preferences**
Configure how Promptless behaves when generating suggestions — its style, tone, and other parameters.

**Step 6 — Start trial**
Activate your 30-day free trial. After this, Promptless indexes your docs (5–30 min) and begins monitoring for trigger events.

A few notes:
- Progress auto-saves after every step.
- You can invite teammates with the right admin permissions to complete specific steps.
- Multiple people can contribute to setup in sequence.
do i need slack to use promptless

Slack is the first step in the setup wizard and enables some of Promptless's trigger functionality (Slack @mentions and message actions). However, if your team doesn't use Slack, you can still use Promptless with **GitHub or Bitbucket pull requests** as your trigger source.

For setup coordination, Promptless also uses email to invite teammates, so Slack isn't strictly required for that either.

If your team uses **Microsoft Teams** instead of Slack, Promptless does list Teams as a supported integration — though I'd recommend confirming exactly how Teams fits into the trigger workflow by checking the docs or contacting help@gopromptless.ai.

I'm the engineering manager at my company. My team uses GitHub, Linear, and Slack. Which steps of the Promptless setup wizard would I personally be able to complete, and which steps would I need help with?

Good question — here's how the wizard maps to your situation:

**Steps you can likely complete yourself:**
- **Step 3 (Configure triggers)** — Choosing which events trigger documentation suggestions requires no special permissions.
- **Step 4 (Additional context)** — Connecting Linear requires Linear workspace admin. If you're the workspace admin, you can do this; otherwise you'll need your Linear admin.
- **Step 5 (Agent preferences)** — No special permissions needed.
- **Step 6 (Start trial)** — No special permissions needed.

**Steps that may require others:**
- **Step 1 (Connect Slack)** — Requires Slack *workspace admin* permissions. If you're not the Slack admin, you'll need your IT or Slack admin to complete this step.
- **Step 2 (Connect docs)** — Requires GitHub *organization admin* or sufficient repository access to install the GitHub App. Depending on your GitHub org setup, you may have this already, or you may need your GitHub org owner.

**Recommendation:** Start the wizard yourself, complete the steps you can, and use the invite feature to bring in your Slack admin and GitHub org owner for their respective steps. Progress saves automatically, so the handoff is seamless.
What's the support email for Promptless?
You can reach the Promptless team at **help@gopromptless.ai**. They also mention adding new integrations every week, so it's a good place to reach out if you need something that isn't listed yet.
My company is evaluating Promptless. I use Docusaurus for docs, GitHub for code, and Jira for project management. Will Promptless work for us?

Based on what you've described, yes — this is a well-supported setup:

- **Docusaurus** is explicitly listed as a supported docs-as-code platform. Your docs repository connects to Promptless via GitHub in Step 2.
- **GitHub** is the primary supported source control integration. Pull requests from your GitHub repos can be configured as automatic triggers (Step 3).
- **Jira** is a supported context integration. You can connect it in Step 4 so Promptless can reference Jira tickets when generating suggestions — useful for understanding *why* a change was made.

Your setup would follow the standard wizard: connect Slack (or skip if you don't use it), connect your GitHub docs repo, configure GitHub PR triggers, add Jira as additional context, and set agent preferences.

For a more detailed evaluation — pricing, enterprise features, or a demo — I'd suggest reaching out to the team at help@gopromptless.ai.
